change_summary: Since c2 (2026-04-08), detail_evidence shrank ~58% (2808->1192; the c2-flagged duplicate block was removed and text compressed) and detail_verdict_text grew ~11x (85->953; expanded with the Part 3.1b trivial-transcription framing and a reference to a hardcoded-expected-value-in-the-same-commit-as-a-live-fetch-call argument). Additionally, a substantive new dome-side domain ("P-wave Shadow Zone" in 08-live-power.txt) now exists that was not captured in the c2 source-material review.
our_argument_summary: Std Model Explains. P-wave shadow zone at 104-140 deg is foundational standard-seismology evidence for a spherical, layered Earth with a liquid outer core (Oldham 1906, Gutenberg 1913, Lehmann 1936). S-waves blocked, P-waves refract as PKP; the angular boundaries are derived from Snell-law refraction on concentric spherical layers. Dome provides no derivation from disc geometry. The detail_verdict_text now also folds in the Part 3.1b trivial-transcription/post-hoc argument.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Dome now lists a "Domain: P-wave Shadow Zone" in its live-power audit panel with Predicted: Shadow at 104-140 deg | Observed: Confirmed at depth 378 km | Tolerance: N/A | Status: PASS and Source: USGS FDSN. No derivation from disc/firmament parameters is provided anywhere in raw-text. The "Observed: Confirmed at depth 378 km" value is physically meaningless as a shadow-zone confirmation (shadow zones are angular, not depth measurements) and suggests a display-only panel rather than a real comparison.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Almost certainly hardcoded/display-only: "Tolerance: N/A" plus a static "Confirmed at depth 378 km" observation is inconsistent with any live pred-vs-obs comparison. Without access to monitor.py in this session I cannot definitively distinguish hardcoded vs no-code, but "none" as c2 claimed is no longer defensible given that 08-live-power.txt now explicitly lists the domain with a PASS status.
data_sources_used: USGS FDSN (claimed in dome panel, not independently verified)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: "Tolerance: N/A" is itself an escape clause: without a tolerance band, no observed value can ever falsify the claim. The dome asserts Status: PASS without specifying under what quantitative condition it would be FAIL.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: The current code_analysis.monitoring = "none" tag on wins.json is stale. The dome has added a live-power Domain entry for this WIN since c2. The correct tag is almost certainly "hardcoded" (static PASS with N/A tolerance and a nonsensical depth-not-angle observation). Separately, the detail_verdict_text asserts "the hardcoded expected value sits in the same commit as the live fetch call," which implies live_fetch — this directly contradicts the monitoring: "none" tag stored on the same WIN. Either the text or the tag is wrong; both cannot be right.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: 08-live-power.txt line 244-245 lists Domain: P-wave Shadow Zone with Predicted: Shadow at 104-140 deg | Observed: Confirmed at depth 378 km | Tolerance: N/A | Status: PASS. A tolerance of N/A plus a static textual Observed value (and a depth value that is physically unrelated to angular shadow zones) is the signature of a hardcoded display panel, not a live pred-vs-obs comparison. The stored tag "none" is out of date — the domain now has a panel, even if that panel appears fake.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Unchanged from c2. 104-140 deg shadow zone is textbook seismology since Oldham 1906 / Gutenberg 1913. The dome takes the observation wholesale, labels it PASS, and offers no alternative derivation.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Unchanged from c2. Observation established ~120 years ago; WIN-064 added in dome V51.0 (April 2026). The numerical boundary is textbook physics, satisfying Part 3.1b trivial-transcription test — exactly as the new detail_verdict_text argues.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Unchanged from c2. No derivation exists anywhere in raw-text that takes disc_radius, H(r), or other dome parameters and produces the 104-140 deg boundary. The dome panel simply restates the spherical-seismology value.

holes_found.description: Code-analysis tag is now stale and internally inconsistent with WIN-064 prose. The stored code_analysis.monitoring = "none" dates from when c2 searched the raw-text and found no dome-side domain. 08-live-power.txt now contains a dedicated "Domain: P-wave Shadow Zone" panel with Predicted/Observed/Tolerance/Status fields and a Source: USGS FDSN label. Simultaneously, detail_verdict_text asserts "the hardcoded expected value sits in the same commit as the live fetch call" — a live_fetch framing. A defender screenshotting the tag vs. the verdict text can credibly argue that our review does not know what the dome is doing. This also cascades into Part 2b aggregate counts (e.g., "X/71 have monitoring: none") that are now off by one.
holes_found.recommendation: Flip code_analysis.monitoring to "hardcoded" (preferred, given Tolerance: N/A + static depth-mismatched Observed string), or "live_fetch" only if a direct inspection of monitor.py confirms an actual USGS FDSN call that emits the Observed value. Make detail_verdict_text consistent with whichever tag is chosen. Update Part 2b aggregate counts to match.

holes_found.description: Our WIN-064 detail block misses the strongest attack on the dome version — the one our own sections.json Part 6 already makes. Part 6 prose says: "the P-wave shadow zone is observed at all azimuths from any earthquake — it forms a perfect ring around the epicenter regardless of direction. A wall at a fixed geographic location (the dome's bottom plate) would cast a shadow in specific directions, not a symmetric ring." This azimuthal-symmetry argument is geometrically devastating and does not depend on sphere-vs-disc semantics. The WIN-064 detail_evidence never references it, so a reader who sees only the WIN panel gets the weaker "shadow-zone math assumes a sphere" argument instead of the irrefutable "a fixed wall cannot produce a symmetric ring around every epicenter worldwide" argument.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a sentence or two to detail_evidence (or to detail_extra) that states the azimuthal-symmetry point and cross-references Part 6. Example: "The shadow zone forms a symmetric ring at 104-140 deg around every earthquake, regardless of epicenter location or azimuth. Any fixed geographic feature — including the dome's bottom plate — would cast a directional shadow, not a symmetric ring. See Part 6." This is the single biggest missed-argument in the current panel.

holes_found.description: Unverified direct quote of "Sub-Terrestrial Wall." sections.json Part 6 attributes to the dome (in title-case, quote-style) a Sub-Terrestrial Wall mechanism that creates the shadow zone. I could not find that exact phrase anywhere in the archived raw-text. The closest match is lowercase "Sub-terrestrial structure" in 08-live-power.txt line 152, which appears in the Mascon Gravity ↔ SAA Boundary correlation row — a different context entirely (it is not explicitly linked to shadow-zone refraction). The dome site may use "Sub-Terrestrial Wall" on a page not captured in our raw-text archive, but until we verify, this phrasing is at risk of a strawman accusation from a dome defender.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) locate the exact dome-site source for "Sub-Terrestrial Wall" and cite it, or (b) soften the attribution to "the dome's (implied) bottom-boundary structure" without quote marks. The azimuthal-symmetry argument survives either way — it does not depend on the exact name of the mechanism.

holes_found.description: We do not use the dome's own "Observed: Confirmed at depth 378 km" string against it. A shadow zone is an angular phenomenon (104-140 deg); reporting it as a depth measurement is a category error. "Confirmed at depth 378 km" plus "Tolerance: N/A" plus "Status: PASS" is a textbook example of the dome's live-power panels being cosmetic rather than falsifiable, and this particular instance is especially damning because the Observed value is not even in the right units. Our detail block never quotes this — we lose the easiest screenshot-dunk available.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a sentence to detail_evidence quoting the panel verbatim and pointing out that the Observed value is in km (depth) while the Predicted value is in degrees (angular distance). This also strengthens our broader Part 2b argument that dome live-power panels are displays, not validators.

holes_found.description: The c2-flagged "11,600-15,600 km surface distance" parenthetical is still present and still uses sphere math inside a flat-disc rebuttal. Re-verified computation on sphere (R=6,371 km): 104 deg -> 11,564 km, 140 deg -> 15,567 km — rounded values 11,600/15,600 are correct. The adversarial point remains that the parenthetical implicitly borrows spherical geometry to criticize the flat-disc adoption of spherical geometry; readers may find this circular unless we flag it as "these are sphere-model distances, which is exactly the point."
holes_found.recommendation: Add a six-word clarifier: "(using sphere math — which is the point)" after the parenthetical. Cheap fix; preempts the confused-reader objection.

holes_found.description: Finding text still says "proves." c2 flagged this as a judgment call and kept it. On re-read, I agree with c2 — in a summary-row field this close to proof, "proves" is defensible. Noting only for completeness. No change recommended.

stronger_arguments.description: Inner core (Lehmann 1936) — a SECOND concentric spherical boundary inside the outer core was discovered from PKiKP arrivals, which require a solid inner core within the liquid outer core. The dome model has to explain not just one refraction boundary but a nested pair, both derived from shadow-zone/PKP geometry on a sphere. c2 raised this in stronger_arguments; it is still not folded into detail_evidence.
sources: Lehmann, I. (1936). P'. Publications du Bureau Central Seismologique International, Serie A, 14: 87-115.

stronger_arguments.description: Travel-time curves (ak135 / Jeffreys-Bullen): the full family of seismic phases — P, S, PP, SS, PcP, ScS, PKP, PKiKP, PKIKP — fits spherical ray-tracing through PREM to <1% over tens of thousands of station-epicenter pairs. A dome defender can hand-wave one phase; they cannot hand-wave the entire interlocking system. c2 raised this too; still not used.

Tolerance: N/A as a falsifiability violation. The dome's own framing is that "Each domain has explicit tolerance bands. If prediction falls outside tolerance, it fails — no exceptions." (08-live-power.txt "HOW THIS WORKS"). But the P-wave Shadow Zone panel has Tolerance: N/A. By the dome's own self-imposed criterion, this domain is unfalsifiable and should not count toward the accuracy denominator. This is a direct self-contradiction we can use — and it generalizes across all N/A-tolerance panels.
sources: raw-text/08-live-power.txt lines 163-174 (HOW THIS WORKS) and lines 244-245 (P-wave Shadow Zone domain).

kernel_of_truth.description: Any serious Earth model must address the shadow zone. The dome model at least acknowledges the phenomenon exists and pins a specific angular range (104-140 deg) to it, which is more than most competing heterodox cosmologies do. Engaging with the data is a real baseline of intellectual seriousness.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Engagement is necessary but not sufficient. The dome quotes the values without deriving them, reports an Observed value in the wrong units, omits a tolerance, and provides no mechanism that could explain azimuthal symmetry. Acknowledging the observation while co-opting the spherical explanation is not an alternative cosmology — it is parasitism on the standard model.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A thoughtful dome defender would argue: (1) WIN-064 is not a claim that the dome derives the 104-140 deg boundary from first principles — it is a claim that the disc cosmology is not incompatible with the observation (i.e., nothing in the dome model forbids seismic shadow zones). (2) The Observed: Confirmed at depth 378 km may be shorthand for the Gutenberg discontinuity / upper-mantle transition (the ~400 km discontinuity is a real feature). (3) Tolerance: N/A means the claim is deterministic — you either see a shadow at 104-140 deg or you do not, and you do. (4) Our "azimuthal symmetry refutes a fixed wall" attack assumes the dome model commits to a fixed-wall mechanism; if the dome instead invokes a radially symmetric sub-terrestrial structure (which is what "Sub-terrestrial structure" plausibly means), symmetry is preserved.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Pre-empt each leg: (1) A model that "is not incompatible with" an observation has zero predictive content; we should say so explicitly — the dome scoreboard counts this as a confirmed prediction, not a non-contradiction. (2) If "depth 378 km" is shorthand for the 410 km discontinuity, then the dome domain is measuring the wrong thing (depth of a velocity jump, not an angular shadow); we should quote the string and point out the unit mismatch. (3) "Tolerance: N/A = deterministic" is only acceptable if the prediction is qualitative (shadow exists yes/no); the dome elsewhere declares precise boundaries of 104-140 deg, which are quantitative — inconsistent framings. (4) On the azimuthal-symmetry attack, we should state explicitly that the dome's own raw-text references "Sub-terrestrial structure" in a radially symmetric (disc-centered) frame, which does NOT reproduce observer-centered azimuthal symmetry: symmetry around the disc's center does not imply symmetry around every observer's epicenter. This preempts the "radial = symmetric" confusion.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: Compatible. WIN-067 (Antarctic gravity hole), WIN-063 (internal structure), and WIN-054 (El Gordo cluster) are all grouped as "relabels standard spherical evidence" in sections.json Part 6 — same pattern, same critique. Our Part 6 prose lists WIN-054, WIN-064, WIN-067 together as examples where the dome adopts a spherical-geometry result and tags it as disc evidence. No cross-WIN contradictions found. Note: Part 3.1b trivial-transcription framing in the WIN-064 detail_verdict is the same framing used for WIN-035, WIN-039, WIN-067 and is internally consistent.

claims_checked: Angular 104 deg -> surface distance on sphere of radius 6371 km
Angular 140 deg -> surface distance on sphere of radius 6371 km
quantitative_verification.computations: Computed arc = R * pi * angle/180 with R=6371 km. 104 deg -> 11564 km (our text rounds to 11,600 — OK within rounding). 140 deg -> 15567 km (our text rounds to 15,600 — OK). PREM depths 2891 and 5150 are standard reference values.

deeper_analysis: The substantive change since c2 is twofold. First, the dome added a live-power Domain entry for P-wave Shadow Zone (08-live-power.txt) that c2 explicitly said did not exist — which means the stored code_analysis.monitoring tag is now wrong and Part 2b aggregate counts downstream may be off. Second, our detail_verdict_text acquired a Part 3.1b trivial-transcription / hardcoded-in-same-commit-as-live-fetch argument that implicitly contradicts the stored tag. These two contradictions (tag-vs-new-dome-content, tag-vs-our-own-text) are the most defender-exploitable issues in the current state. Everything else (parenthetical sphere math, "proves," unverified "Sub-Terrestrial Wall" quote) is secondary. The verdict "Std Model Explains" is still correct and if anything better supported after we add the azimuthal-symmetry argument to the WIN panel itself. This is a minor_edit, not a major_rewrite — the skeleton is right; the tag needs to be re-synchronized with the dome's current content and with our own verdict prose, and the strongest attack (azimuthal symmetry) needs to be imported from sections.json into the WIN panel.

citations_verified: PREM core-mantle boundary 2891 km and inner-core boundary 5150 km — standard reference values, no DOI required.
104 -> 11,564 km and 140 -> 15,567 km arc distances on R=6371 km sphere — computed, matches rounded values in text.
citations_unchecked: Gutenberg 1913, Physikalische Zeitschrift 14:1217-1218 — inline citation, no DOI; cannot access 1913 journal archive this cycle.
Lehmann 1936 Publications du Bureau Central Seismologique International Serie A, 14:87-115 — in stronger_arguments, not in the WIN text; unchecked for the same reason as Gutenberg.
Kennett & Engdahl 1991 GJI 105:429-465 — in stronger_arguments; unchecked (DOI not cited).
Dome-site attribution of "Sub-Terrestrial Wall" — could not locate exact phrasing in archived raw-text; needs live site verification.
